diagnosis_or_reason: Cellulitis (skin infection) of the right lower leg

medications.name: Amoxicillin
medications.source_quote: Amoxicillin 500 mg by mouth twice daily

medications.name: Acetaminophen
medications.source_quote: Acetaminophen 500 mg every 6 hours as needed

follow_ups.source_quote: Schedule a follow-up visit with your primary care provider within 2–3 days

red_flags.text: Rapidly spreading redness or red streaks up the leg
Severe pain or swelling despite medications

restrictions: No lifting over 10 lbs for 3 days

plain_language_summary: You have a skin infection on your lower leg. Take antibiotics exactly as prescribed, rest with your leg elevated, and follow up with your doctor in a few days. Get urgent care if you develop a high fever, spreading redness, or worse pain.

todays_checklist.label: Take amoxicillin with breakfast and dinner
